name: University
description_short: University Credit Union Center is a 7,600-seat athletic facility on the UC Davis campus, not a consumer finance company.
description_long:
University Credit Union Center is a multi-purpose indoor stadium located on the University of California, Davis campus in Yolo County, California. Originally opened in 1977 as Recreation Hall, the facility was renamed The Pavilion at the ARC in 2004 when it was fused with the newly constructed Activities and Recreation Center. In July 2021, the venue became the University Credit Union Center as part of a 10-year sponsorship agreement with University Credit Union, a financial institution serving the UC Davis community.

The facility operates as a venue for UC Davis Aggies athletic programs, including men's basketball, women's basketball, women's volleyball, and women's gymnastics. The center has a seating capacity of 7,600 (with 6,003 seats for basketball and volleyball events) and provides 150,000 square feet of floor space. The venue features a maple hardwood floor and includes modern amenities such as a Daktronics videoboard added as part of the University Credit Union sponsorship agreement.

The University Credit Union Center distinguishes itself as a university-owned and operated facility integrated into campus life, hosting not only intercollegiate athletics but also intramural sports, public events, and large community gatherings. The facility has hosted notable events including a 2008 political rally for Hillary Clinton that drew approximately 7,800 attendees, as well as World Wrestling Entertainment shows.

It is important to note that University Credit Union Center is an athletic venue and facility, not a financial services provider or consumer finance company. While the naming rights belong to University Credit Union (a credit union serving UC Davis), the center itself offers no financial products, lending services, or credit-related services to consumers. Any consumer seeking financial services would need to contact University Credit Union directly, not this facility. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Large public event hosting and venue rental
Seating capacity for 6,000+ attendees for basketball events
Video display and game statistics via Daktronics videoboard
Multi-purpose indoor facility space (150,000 square feet)
pros: Named sponsor University Credit Union offers PAL (Payday Alternative Loan) products typical of credit unions
Large capacity venue (7,600 seats) accommodates major community events beyond athletics
Recently renovated with modern technology including Daktronics videoboard
Integrated with UC Davis campus, serving students and community members
Multi-purpose facility hosts diverse events including athletics, wrestling entertainment, and political rallies
cons: University Credit Union Center is a physical venue/building, not a financial services company or lender
No consumer finance products or services are offered at this facility location
Website provides no information about financial services, lending terms, rates, or eligibility
The sponsorship agreement does not make this venue a point of service for University Credit Union financial products
best_for: UC Davis students and faculty seeking athletic event venues
Community members attending large public events in the Davis area
Organizations hosting multi-purpose events requiring large indoor capacity
diagnosis: This entry is miscategorized and does not represent a consumer finance company. University Credit Union Center is an athletic facility and event venue on the UC Davis campus. While it bears the name of University Credit Union (a financial institution), the center itself offers no financial products or services. Users seeking University Credit Union's financial services should contact the credit union directly, not this facility.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
